ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Menli …õhye m…î lile arelemgbunli w…î Yid…îm kolaa na Yizilay…õma anyia arelemgbunli azie b…õ. Biy…î ara Bila m…î…î vi Denehabaa Zilaa ara Dwobabe m…î…î vi B…îzelaa Huhyam m…î…î vi Timan la Bidade ara Heedade m…î…î hunle Medeanema w…î Mowabe kp…îk…õ nu la. ∆Üvi Evete. Samelaa m…î…î vi Maselika Hy…î…îlo m…î…î vi Rilih…îbo m…î…î w…î Azule Yufileti ne anloa la. Akeb…î ara Beeyale Heenan Heedade m…î…î vi Peewu, M…õh…õtab…õle …î nli a le Meetel…õde y…õ…õ …î nla a le Meezahabe.
translated: Menli is the name of the place where the people of Yid…îm live and Yizilay…õma people also live there.  Biy…î ara Bila is the name of the place where Denehabaa Zilaa lives. Dwobabe is the name of the place where B…îzelaa Huhyam lives. Timan is the name of the place where Bidade lives. Heedade is the name of the place where Medeanema lives in Mowabe.  Evete. Samelaa is the name of the place where Maselika Hy…î…îlo lives. Rilih…îbo lives in Azule Yufileti and anloa. Akeb…î ara Beeyale Heenan Heedade is the name of the place where Peewu lives, M…õh…õtab…õle is the name of the place where Meetel…õde lives. Meezahabe is the name of the place where the people live.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: na …îhyele b…õ …îguale efiade w…î sinzavol…õma safohyenle ne sua nu …õk…õ ne efiade ne …õleka m…î…î Dwosefi la la.
translated: The one who is not able to stand firm in the face of the challenges of life and who is easily swayed by the opinions of others, is like a tree that is easily uprooted.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: ∆êd…õnk…õma soanvol…õ h…îdole ye w…î …õs…õl…õ ne azo nzubula bie m…î…î w…î Hyulu adenle zo la anwo,
translated: The great gathering of the people was held in the midst of the forest where they were to perform the ritual of sacrifice to the god of the forest.

ref: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
text: ∆êlal…õ fane nwi…î ne m…î…î Nana hanle la kile k…õ Nyamenle …õb…î ye kp…îk…õ …õwie, na …înr…õhy…õ biala na yeazi.
translated: When the king saw the people, he said to them, "I have heard that Nyamenle has made a great noise, and he has been boasting."

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: S…õnz…õne ne …õl…õk…î ah…îd…î la, nafel…õ kpole vale Ebilam, na awozinli kpole anu …õzulol…õ bie rahedale …î nwo zo.
translated: When the old man and the elephant met at the crossroads, the young man quickly went to Ebilam, and the woman quickly went to the place where they were going to meet.
